_note: Batch b_1000BC_0_2 — documented parents (and full siblings) for married-in spouses that had none. All HISTORICAL unless flagged. NEW parent/sibling nodes use the SPC_ prefix; existing spouses are referenced by id STRING so the build merely appends them as children (never re-parents). Two families reuse pre-existing parents: LAODICE4 is appended under the EXISTING Seleucid pair ANTIOCHUS3 ● LAODICE3 (she was a full sister-wife of Seleucus IV). SPC_SERVILIA is one shared node used as the mother in BOTH the Junia-Tertia union (● D. Junius Silanus) and the Brutus union (● M. Junius Brutus the Elder) — Servilia's real two marriages. Unknown mothers drawn as '—' placeholders, never invented. SKIPPED (undocumented/legendary parentage, reported separately): ATT_APOLLONIS, MAC_POLYCRATIA, GRK_MENANDER, GRK_AGATHOKLEIA, JUL_MARCIA, JUL_ATIUSBALBUS, JUL_TERTULLA, JUL_TERENTIA, SALOMEALEX, GAIUSOCTAVIUS, ZECHARIAH, JUL_CASSIUS, JUL_TIBCLAUDIUSNERO. Web/scholar-verified July 2026 against Wikipedia entries for each figure named in the notes.

families.note: Laodice IV was a daughter of Antiochus III the Great & Laodice III and the sister-wife of Seleucus IV — appended as a sibling under the EXISTING Seleucid parents. Wikipedia 'Laodice IV'.
Ariarathes IV was son of Ariarathes III of Cappadocia & the Seleucid Stratonice (daughter of Antiochus II Theos). Diodorus; Wikipedia 'Ariarathes III'/'Ariarathes IV'.

a.label:
👑 Ariarathes III of Cappadocia
c.275-220 BC · King of Cappadocia, first of the house to take the royal title; father of Ariarathes IV · historical

b.label:
Stratonice
c.265 BC · Seleucid princess, daughter of Antiochus II Theos; wife of Ariarathes III & mother of Ariarathes IV · historical

families.note: Tigranes the Great is named as son of Tigranes I in the ancient sources (Appian, Justin); the early Artaxiad succession is debated — flagged.

a.label:
👑 Tigranes I of Armenia
c.170-95 BC · Artaxiad king of Armenia, named in the sources as father of Tigranes the Great · ⚠ early Artaxiad descent debated · historical

families.note: Huhanye & his rival Zhizhi were both sons of Xulüquanqu Chanyu. Book of Han; Wikipedia 'Huhanye'/'Zhizhi Chanyu'.

a.label:
👑 Xulüquanqu Chanyu
d.60 BC · ruler of the Xiongnu r.68-60 BC; father of Huhanye & Zhizhi · historical

children.label:
👑 Zhizhi Chanyu
d.36 BC · Xiongnu ruler & brother of Huhanye; rival chanyu who broke west & was destroyed by a Han army in 36 BC · historical

families.note: Juba II was the son of King Juba I of Numidia, paraded as an infant in Caesar's triumph. Plutarch; Wikipedia 'Juba II'.

a.label:
👑 Juba I of Numidia
c.85-46 BC · King of Numidia; ally of Pompey against Caesar, took his own life after Thapsus (46 BC); father of Juba II · historical

families.note: Aurelia Cotta was daughter of L. Aurelius Cotta & Rutilia; her brothers were the consuls Gaius (75 BC), Marcus (74 BC) & Lucius (65 BC) Aurelius Cotta. Wikipedia 'Aurelia (mother of Caesar)'.

a.label:
Lucius Aurelius Cotta
c.150 BC · Roman senator of the consular gens Aurelia; father of Aurelia & the three Cotta brothers · historical

b.label:
Rutilia
c.145 BC · wife of Lucius Aurelius Cotta & mother of Aurelia · historical

children.label:
Gaius Aurelius Cotta
c.124-73 BC · orator & consul 75 BC; brother of Aurelia, mother of Caesar · historical

Marcus Aurelius Cotta
c.120 BC · consul 74 BC; brother of Aurelia · historical

Lucius Aurelius Cotta
c.115 BC · consul 65 BC & censor; brother of Aurelia · historical

families.note: Mucia Tertia was daughter of Q. Mucius Scaevola Pontifex (cos 95 BC) & Licinia. Wikipedia 'Mucia Tertia'.

a.label:
Quintus Mucius Scaevola 'Pontifex'
c.140-82 BC · Pontifex Maximus & consul 95 BC, the great jurist; father of Mucia Tertia · historical

b.label:
Licinia
c.115 BC · wife of Q. Mucius Scaevola & mother of Mucia Tertia · historical

families.note: Cornelia was daughter of the four-time consul L. Cornelius Cinna & Annia; her brother was the praetor L. Cornelius Cinna. Wikipedia 'Cornelia (wife of Caesar)'.

a.label:
Lucius Cornelius Cinna
c.130-84 BC · four-time consul (87-84 BC) & leader of the Marian faction; father of Caesar's first wife Cornelia · historical

b.label:
Annia
c.110 BC · wife of Lucius Cornelius Cinna & mother of Cornelia · historical

children.label:
Lucius Cornelius Cinna
c.100 BC · praetor 44 BC; brother of Cornelia & brother-in-law of Julius Caesar · historical

families.note: Atilia was daughter of Aulus Atilius Serranus; mother unrecorded. Plutarch, Cato Minor 7.

a.label:
Aulus Atilius Serranus
c.115 BC · Roman senator of the gens Atilia; father of Atilia, first wife of Cato the Younger · historical

families.note: Junia Tertia & her sisters Junia Prima & Junia Secunda were daughters of D. Junius Silanus (cos 62 BC) & Servilia. Wikipedia 'Junia Tertia'/'Servilia'.

a.label:
Decimus Junius Silanus
c.100-60 BC · consul 62 BC; second husband of Servilia & father of the three Juniae · historical

b.label:
Servilia
c.100-42 BC · influential noblewoman & mistress of Julius Caesar; mother of the assassin Brutus (by M. Junius Brutus the Elder) & of the three Juniae (by D. Junius Silanus) · historical

children.label:
Junia Prima
c.85 BC · eldest of the three Juniae; wife of P. Servilius Isauricus · historical

Junia Secunda
c.80 BC · a Junia; wife of the triumvir M. Aemilius Lepidus · historical

families.note: The assassin Brutus was son of M. Junius Brutus the Elder & Servilia — SPC_SERVILIA reused as his mother (her first marriage). Wikipedia 'Marcus Junius Brutus'.

a.label:
Marcus Junius Brutus the Elder
d.77 BC · tribune 83 BC, first husband of Servilia & father of the assassin Brutus; killed by Pompey · historical

families.note: Livia Drusilla was daughter of M. Livius Drusus Claudianus & Aufidia. Wikipedia 'Livia'.

a.label:
Marcus Livius Drusus Claudianus
c.90-42 BC · praetor, born a Claudius & adopted by the Livii Drusi; father of the empress Livia; died after Philippi · historical

b.label:
Aufidia
c.75 BC · daughter of Aufidius Lurco; wife of M. Livius Drusus Claudianus & mother of Livia · historical

families.note: The triumvir Crassus was the youngest son of the consul P. Licinius Crassus (cos 97 BC); his two elder brothers perished in the Marian purge of 87 BC, and he married Tertulla, a brother's widow. Plutarch, Crassus 1, 4-6.

a.label:
Publius Licinius Crassus
c.140-87 BC · consul 97 BC & censor; father of the triumvir Crassus; died in the Marian terror · historical

children.label:
Publius Licinius Crassus
c.116-87 BC · eldest son of the consul; died with his father in the Marian purge · historical

Lucius Licinius Crassus
c.114 BC · son of the consul; died in the Marian purge of 87 BC · historical

families.note: L. Domitius Ahenobarbus (cos 16 BC) was son of Gnaeus Domitius Ahenobarbus (cos 32 BC) — distinct from his own son DOMITIUSAHEN (cos 32 AD, Nero's father). Suetonius, Nero 3-4.

a.label:
Gnaeus Domitius Ahenobarbus
c.75-31 BC · consul 32 BC; Antony's admiral who defected to Octavian before Actium; father of the consul of 16 BC · historical

families.note: Pompey the Great was son of Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo (cos 89 BC) & Lucilia; his sister Pompeia married C. Memmius. Wikipedia 'Pompey'/'Pompeius Strabo'.

a.label:
Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo
c.135-87 BC · consul 89 BC in the Social War; father of Pompey the Great; died in the siege of Rome · historical

b.label:
Lucilia
c.120 BC · of the gens Lucilia; wife of Pompeius Strabo & mother of Pompey the Great · historical

children.label:
Pompeia
c.100 BC · sister of Pompey the Great; wife of Gaius Memmius · historical

families.note: Cato the Younger & his sister Porcia were children of M. Porcius Cato & Livia Drusa (his sister Porcia is distinct from his daughter Porcia, JUL_PORCIA). Plutarch, Cato Minor 1.

a.label:
Marcus Porcius Cato
c.130-91 BC · of the gens Porcia, grandson of Cato the Elder; father of Cato the Younger · historical

b.label:
Livia (Drusa)
c.115 BC · sister of the reformer M. Livius Drusus; mother of Cato the Younger (by Cato) & of Servilia (by an earlier marriage) · historical

children.label:
Porcia
c.93 BC · sister of Cato the Younger; wife of L. Domitius Ahenobarbus (cos 54 BC) · historical

families.note: Cicero & his brother Quintus were sons of M. Tullius Cicero the Elder & Helvia. Plutarch, Cicero 1.

a.label:
Marcus Tullius Cicero the Elder
c.140-64 BC · a well-to-do eques of Arpinum; father of the orator Cicero & of Quintus · historical

b.label:
Helvia
c.120 BC · of the gens Helvia; wife of the elder Cicero & mother of the orator · historical

children.label:
Quintus Tullius Cicero
102-43 BC · younger brother of the orator; praetor & Caesar's legate in Gaul; proscribed with his brother · historical

families.note: Fulvia was daughter of M. Fulvius Bambalio & Sempronia. Cicero, Philippics; Wikipedia 'Fulvia'.

a.label:
Marcus Fulvius Bambalio
c.100 BC · a Roman of Tusculum of little note (Cicero mocked his stammer); father of Fulvia · historical

b.label:
Sempronia
c.95 BC · of the gens Sempronia; wife of Fulvius Bambalio & mother of Fulvia · historical

families.note: Mark Antony & his brothers Gaius & Lucius were sons of M. Antonius Creticus & Julia, daughter of L. Julius Caesar (cos 90 BC). Plutarch, Antony 1.

a.label:
Marcus Antonius Creticus
d.c.71 BC · praetor 74 BC, son of the orator M. Antonius; given a failed command against the Cretan pirates; father of the triumvir Mark Antony · historical

b.label:
Julia
c.104 BC · daughter of Lucius Julius Caesar (cos 90 BC), a kinswoman of the Caesars; mother of Mark Antony & his brothers · historical

children.label:
Gaius Antonius
d.42 BC · brother of Mark Antony; captured by the Liberators & executed after Philippi · historical

Lucius Antonius
fl.44-40 BC · youngest brother of Mark Antony; consul 41 BC, led the Perusine War against Octavian · historical

families.note: Marcus Agrippa had an elder brother Lucius & a sister Vipsania Polla; their father was Lucius Vipsanius Agrippa. Wikipedia 'Marcus Vipsanius Agrippa'.

a.label:
Lucius Vipsanius Agrippa
c.90 BC · of an obscure plebeian family; father of Augustus' admiral Marcus Vipsanius Agrippa · historical

children.label:
Lucius Vipsanius Agrippa
c.65 BC · elder brother of Marcus Agrippa; fought for the Pompeians & was pardoned at his brother's request · historical

Vipsania Polla
c.60 BC · sister of Marcus Agrippa · historical

families.note: Scribonia was daughter of L. Scribonius Libo & sister of the consul L. Scribonius Libo (cos 34 BC). Wikipedia 'Scribonia'.

a.label:
Lucius Scribonius Libo
c.100 BC · Roman senator of the gens Scribonia; father of Scribonia (wife of Augustus) & of the consul Libo · historical

children.label:
Lucius Scribonius Libo
c.90-21 BC · consul 34 BC; brother of Scribonia & father-in-law of Sextus Pompeius · historical
